originalTitle: Kenyan Sawe smashes two-hour barrier to make history in London
title: Our African Hero Runs Super Fast! Sabastian Sawe Makes History!
content:
Hello, Little Superstars!

Hey there, amazing kids! Your friend from African News is here with super exciting news! Guess what? Something truly wonderful happened. It made all of Africa, especially Kenya, burst with joy! We have a new hero to celebrate.

Meet Our Hero: Sabastian Sawe!

We have a new hero! His name is Sabastian Sawe. Can you say that with me? Sa-bas-tian Sa-we! He is from our very own continent, from a country called Kenya. Kenya is famous for super-fast runners! Many champions come from there.

What Did Sabastian Do?

Sabastian did something nobody has ever done before in a big, important race. He ran a super, super long race called a marathon. And guess what? He ran it faster than anyone in a *competitive* race! It was less than two hours! Wow!

What is a Marathon?

Did you know what a marathon is? It's a very, very long race! It's like running from your home to a far-off town. The distance is 42 kilometres long! That's a super big distance for your little legs, right? Grown-ups need lots of practice to run so far. **Did you know?** The very first marathon race happened a very, very long time ago in a country called Greece! A brave messenger ran a long way to share important news.

Less Than Two Hours? That's Super Fast!

Imagine running for two whole hours. That's how long some of your favourite cartoons last! But Sabastian ran the *whole* super long marathon in even *less* time than that. He ran for 1 hour and 58 minutes and 24 seconds! That's quicker than quick! This means he ran so fast, he finished the whole race before many people could even make their lunch! He truly flew past all the other runners.

Where Did This Happen?

This amazing race happened far away, in a big city called London. London is in a country called England. Many runners from all over the world go there to race. It's a very famous race called the London Marathon. **Did you know?** The London Marathon has lots of people running, not just the very fastest ones! Many people run to help charities and raise money for good things like helping other children.

Sabastian's Amazing Run

Sabastian started the race with many other fast runners from different countries. They all ran, ran, ran very hard! But Sabastian had a special goal in his heart. He wanted to run super, super fast. He wanted to finish before two hours. He pushed his strong legs. He breathed deeply, in and out, in and out. He thought about his country, Kenya, and all the people watching him. He kept going, step after step after step, never stopping.

Making History!

When Sabastian crossed the finish line, everyone cheered loudly! The clocks showed his amazing time: 1 hour, 58 minutes, 24 seconds. He did it! He smashed the two-hour barrier! This means he ran faster than anyone else has *ever* run a competitive marathon before. He made history! Making history means doing something so special and new that people will remember it for a very, very long time. Just like when you learn to ride your bike for the first time without help – that's a big moment for you!

Why Is This So Special for Africa?

This is super special for all of us in Africa! Sabastian is an African hero. He shows the whole world how strong and fast African people are. He makes all of us very, very proud. When one of us does something amazing, it makes everyone feel happy and proud. It's like when your friend wins a prize at school, you feel happy for them, right? This is even bigger for our whole continent!

Hard Work and Dreams

How do you think Sabastian became so fast? Was it magic? No! It was very, very hard work. Sabastian probably woke up super early every day, even when it was cold. He ran and ran and ran for many, many hours. He ate healthy food to make his body strong and full of energy. He listened to his coaches who taught him how to run even better. He never gave up, even when his legs felt tired and sore. He had a big dream, and he worked very hard to make it come true. **Did you know?** Many runners from Kenya live in places with high mountains. Living high up in the mountains helps their bodies get stronger and better at breathing when they run!

You Can Be a Champion Too!

Sabastian's story tells us something very important. If you work hard, if you practice a lot, and if you believe in yourself, you can achieve amazing things too! Maybe you want to be a great student, or a fantastic artist, or a super football player. Just like Sabastian, you need to practice and never give up. Every little step, every day, makes you stronger and better. Keep trying, keep learning, and your dreams can come true!

Cheering for Our African Star!

So, let's give a big cheer for Sabastian Sawe! He is a true African champion. He ran faster than fast! He made history! And he showed us all that with hard work, dreams really do come true. He is an inspiration for all the little boys and girls across Africa. Go, Sabastian, go!
summary: Sabastian Sawe, a fast runner from Kenya, made history by running a super long race called a marathon in less than two hours, faster than anyone ever has in a big competition.
whyMatters: This is important because Sabastian's amazing run shows the world how strong and fast African runners are, making all of us very proud and inspiring kids to work hard for their dreams.

originalTitle: Syria puts first Assad-era chief on trial in Damascus
title: Justice for Our Friends in Syria!
content:
Hello from Africa!

Hi everyone! Your friendly news reporter from Africa is here today. We have a very important story to share with you. It is about fairness and doing the right thing. This story comes from a country far, far away from us. It is called Syria. Even though it is far, we care about what happens there. We want all children and families to live in peace. Let's learn about this big news together!

A Big Story from Far Away

Syria is a beautiful country. It has many old cities and a long, rich history. It is in a part of the world called the Middle East. People in Syria speak a language called Arabic. Just like we have leaders in our countries in Africa, Syria also has its leaders. Sometimes, things can be difficult in Syria, but people always hope for peace. This news is a step towards making things better for everyone there. *Did you know?* Syria is one of the oldest countries in the world! People have lived there for thousands and thousands of years. Imagine buildings that are even older than your great-grandparents!

Meet Mr. Atef Najib

Now, let's talk about a man named Mr. Atef Najib. He used to have a very important job in Syria. He was in charge of something called "political security." This was in a part of Syria called Deraa province. A 'province' is like a big region or area in a country. Mr. Najib was a powerful person. He made many decisions and was someone many people listened to. But now, things have changed for him. *Did you know?* A 'province' is a bit like a state or a big county. It helps to organize a country and make it easier to manage!

What is "Political Security"?

What does "political security" mean? It sounds like big, grown-up words! Let's make it simple. Imagine you have a special helper whose job is to keep everyone safe and make sure rules are followed. They try to stop bad things from happening. That is what 'security' means. It is about keeping order and peace. But sometimes, people with a lot of power might not use it in a good way. They might make people scared instead of helping them. They might hurt people. This is why it is very important for powerful people to always be fair and kind to everyone.

What are "Crimes Against People"?

Mr. Atef Najib is facing some very serious charges. He is accused of "crimes against the Syrian people." What does this mean? A 'crime' is when someone does something very wrong. It breaks the law. It can hurt others. For example, stealing a toy is a small crime. Hurting someone is a serious crime. "Crimes against the people" means doing very bad things to many, many people. It means actions that made lots of families unhappy or unsafe. These are very serious accusations. People want to know the truth. They want fairness for everyone. *Did you know?* Every country has laws! Laws are like special rules that everyone must follow. They help us all live together safely and fairly!

Going to Court: What is a Trial?

So, what happens when someone is accused of such serious things? They go to a "trial." A trial is like a big, important meeting. It happens in a special place called a court. In the court, special judges listen very carefully. They listen to what happened. They listen to people who saw things or were affected. They listen to what the accused person says. Their job is to decide if the person did the bad things or not. It is a way to find out the truth and make a fair decision. This trial is happening in Damascus. Damascus is the capital city of Syria. It is a very old and famous city, just like some of the big cities we have here in Africa. *Did you know?* A judge is a bit like a referee in a game. They make sure the rules are followed and decide what is fair and right!

Why is This Important?

This trial is very, very important. Mr. Najib is the first person from a special time to go to court like this. He was important when a leader called Mr. Assad was in charge. For a long time, many people in Syria have been waiting for fairness. They want to see that if someone does bad things, they will be asked about it. This trial shows that no one is above the law. It means that even very powerful people must follow the rules. This gives hope to many people in Syria. It tells them that things might start to get better. It shows that justice can happen.

Making Things Fair

When we talk about 'justice', we mean making things fair for everyone. If someone is hurt, justice tries to make things right again. If someone does something wrong, justice means they have to answer for it. This trial is a big step for justice in Syria. It is about saying that bad things should not happen. And if they do, the people responsible should explain themselves and be held accountable. This helps a country heal. It helps people trust again. It helps build a better future for children and families to live in peace. *Did you know?* The idea of 'justice' means everyone should be treated fairly and with respect. No matter who they are or where they come from!

Thinking About Our Friends in Syria

So, that's the big news from Syria. It's a story about a man on trial. It's about finding out the truth. And most importantly, it's about making things fair for all the people in Syria. We hope that this trial brings a sense of peace and fairness to their beautiful country. We send our kind wishes to all the children and families in Syria. May they find peace and happiness in their homes. And remember, trying to be fair and kind is important everywhere, every day!
summary: A man named Atef Najib, who used to be powerful in Syria, is now on trial in court for allegedly doing bad things to many people, which is an important step towards fairness for everyone there.
whyMatters: This trial is very important because it shows that even powerful people must follow the rules, and it brings hope for fairness and a better future for the people of Syria.

originalTitle: Trump cancels US envoys' trip to Pakistan for talks on Iran war
title: Special Messengers and Big Talks!
content:
Hello, Super Friends!

Hi everyone! It's your friendly news reporter for Africa, ready to tell you about some important news from far, far away. We're going to talk about big leaders, special helpers, and why talking is super important for peace. Sometimes, things change, even for grown-ups and big countries. Let's find out what happened!

Big News from Far Away!

Imagine you have a very important leader in your school. In America, they have a very important leader called President Trump. He is like the headmaster of a big school, but for a whole country! President Trump had some special helpers, like special messengers or friends, who were planning a long trip. We call these special helpers "envoys." Their job is to travel and talk to other countries. These special helpers were going to fly all the way to a country called Pakistan. Pakistan is very far away from America and even far from us here in Africa. It's a land with beautiful mountains and kind people. Why were they going to Pakistan? They wanted to have "talks." Talks are like very serious chats. They wanted to talk about some big problems with another country called Iran. But guess what? The trip was cancelled! It means they are not going to Pakistan right now. It's like when you plan to go play with a friend, but then your friend or your parents say, "Not today, maybe another time."

Why Do Countries Talk?

Think of countries like really big families or groups of friends living in different houses. Sometimes, friends have a disagreement. Maybe one friend borrowed a toy and forgot to give it back. What do you do when you have a disagreement with a friend? You talk about it, right? You try to explain how you feel and listen to them. That's how you fix problems and stay friends! Countries do the same thing! They talk to each other to solve big problems. When countries talk, it's called "diplomacy." It's a fancy word for trying to be friendly and sort things out without fighting. **Did you know?** Talking helps countries avoid big fights called wars. Wars are very sad and hurt many people, so talking is always the best way!

Who Are These Special Helpers?

The special helpers from America were two important people. Their names were Steve Witkoff and Jared Kushner. They are like very clever detectives or messengers for President Trump. Their job was to represent America. That means they speak for their country, just like when you choose a friend to speak for your team in a game. They were going to share America's ideas and listen to others. It's a very big responsibility! They needed to be good listeners and good talkers. They wanted to find a way for America and Iran to understand each other better.

Problems with Iran?

So, what were the problems with Iran? Well, sometimes countries have different ideas about how things should work. Imagine two friends who both want to play with the same ball, but in different ways. America and Iran had some big disagreements, like different ideas about important things in the world. These disagreements made people a little worried. When people worry too much, they might think about a "war." A war is a very big, very dangerous fight between countries. Nobody wants a war, because it makes everyone sad and unsafe. That's why these special talks were so important. The special helpers wanted to see if they could help America and Iran talk it out. Talking is like finding a way for both friends to play with the ball without fighting!

Why Was the Trip Cancelled?

So, if talking is so important, why did the trip get cancelled? Iran, the other country, said something important. They said they were not ready for a direct meeting with America right now. "Direct meeting" means talking face-to-face, just America and Iran. It's like when you want to talk to your friend alone, but your friend says, "Can we invite another friend too, or maybe wait a bit?" Maybe Iran wanted more time to think. Maybe they wanted to talk in a different way, or with other countries present. It can be tricky for big countries to agree on how and when to talk. When a trip like this is cancelled, it doesn't mean the problem is gone. It just means they need to try a different plan or wait for a better time to talk. It's like when your soccer game gets rained out – you just have to reschedule! **Did you know?** Even when big meetings are cancelled, leaders often keep talking in other ways, like sending messages or talking through other friendly countries. They never give up on trying to make peace!

Why is Peace Important?

Peace is one of the most wonderful things in the world! When there is peace, everyone feels safe and happy. Children can go to school every day and learn new things. Families can stay together and play. People can work and build amazing houses and roads. When countries are peaceful, they can help each other grow and become stronger. Imagine a world where everyone is smiling and working together. That's what peace looks like! It helps build a better world for all of us, no matter where we live, even here in Africa. **Did you know?** There's a big group called the United Nations, or UN for short. They are like super-helpers for the whole world! They help countries talk to each other and find peaceful ways to solve problems. They try to make sure all children can live in peace.

What Happens Next?

So, what happens now that the special helpers' trip was cancelled? Does it mean they stop trying to make peace? No way! Even when a plan changes, people don't stop trying to find solutions. The leaders and their special helpers will keep thinking of new ways to talk. They will try to find a time and a place where everyone feels ready to chat. It's like when you're trying to build a really tall tower with blocks. Sometimes it falls down, but you don't give up! You try again, maybe in a different way, until you succeed. We all hope that America and Iran will find a good way to talk soon. Because talking is the first step to being friends and having a peaceful world.

Our Wish for Peace

It's so important for all of us, especially children, to know that leaders are always trying to make the world a safer and happier place. Even when it's hard, they keep trying! Let's all send our good wishes for peace and friendship to all the countries in the world. Every little bit of kindness helps! We hope that America and Iran can find a way to talk and build peace together very soon. Thank you for listening, super friends! See you next time for more news that matters!
summary: Important helpers from America had their trip cancelled because a country called Iran wasn't ready to meet, but everyone hopes countries will keep talking to find peace.
whyMatters: It matters because when countries talk, they can solve problems peacefully and avoid big fights, making the world safer for everyone, especially children.
